name: Oil-Water Separation
description: Two immiscible fluids (oil and water) separate under Shan-Chen inter-component repulsion. A high-density oil droplet placed in water spontaneously separates into distinct phases. Demonstrates multiphase interface dynamics and surface tension.

product.lesson_headline: Immiscible fluids spontaneously separate under inter-particle repulsion.
product.export_caption: Oil-water separation: two components form distinct phases.
classroom_prompts: What happens when you change g12 (inter-component repulsion)?
How does the interface sharpness change with sigma?
Why do the phases separate into flat layers instead of droplets?
